svg.text: InnoDB leaf-page locks: heap_no, gap attachment, trx_t and lock bitmap
Page slots define heap_no; trx_t::lock owns lock_t objects whose trailing bitmap marks locked slots

g.text: Leaf page P: logical order (next-record); hatched intervals are gaps owned by the successor heap_no
Key order is independent of heap_no (id=20 occupies slot 4; id=30 occupies slot 3).
Gap before record R is represented by lock bits on R's heap_no (successor attachment).
Physical heap slots on page P
Slots need not be dense or key-ordered
bitmap example (LOCK_X | LOCK_REC_NOT_GAP on id=20, heap_no=4, page P):
Stored on trx->lock.lock_heap (or rec_pool). Not part of the leaf page frame.
Does not block UPDATE of id=20 alone
heap_no indexes the physical record slot on the leaf page. Key order uses next-record pointers.
trx_t::lock owns lock_heap and trx_locks. Each record lock_t is allocated there (or from rec_pool).
Bitmap bytes follow the lock_t header at (byte*)&lock[1]; n_bits is the length; bit[i] marks heap_no i on page_id.
type_mode selects LOCK_REC_NOT_GAP / LOCK_GAP / LOCK_ORDINARY / LOCK_INSERT_INTENTION.
Gap before R uses bits on R's heap_no. Locks are not stored in the index page image.
lock_sys->rec_hash chains lock_t by page_id via lock_t::hash.
